title: [RSS] 2026-005: High Vulnerability in the Linux Kernel ("Copy Fail")
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_advisories_rss:a19c2d96147faf086ec7 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_advisories_rss:a19c2d96147faf086ec7 -->

### Text
On 29 April 2026, a high local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel, tracked as CVE-2026-31431 and named "Copy Fail", was publicly disclosed.
The vulnerability affects every mainstream Linux distributions shipping a kernel built since 2017. A public proof-of-concept exploit has been released.
As of the date of this advisory, no distribution has shipped a fixed kernel package. The mainline fix was committed on 1 April 2026, but vendor updates are still pending across all major distributions. CERT-EU strongly recommends applying the interim mitigation immediately, prioritising Kubernetes nodes, and CI/CD runners exposed to untrusted workloads.

### Zeit (UTC)

### Text
Brazil: Federal Police [PF] seized 183 kg of cocaine in paste form, packed into 169 tabletes hidden in secret compartments of a foreign truck’s cabin and cargo area during a routine check on highway BR-277 near Balsa Nova, Paraná.
Alongside the drug haul, officers found two long guns (fuzis), a revolver, two pistols, and cell phones. The truck driver was arrested on the spot and taken to police headquarters in Curitiba, where he faces charges for international drug trafficking and illegal weapons trafficking.

title: [X] Spain: Three Duarry Supercat high-performance rigid-hulled inflatable boats, designed for military use and each measuring six meters and weighing 640 kg, were stolen from the Navy during Flotex-26 maneuvers at El Retín beach in Barba…
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:worldcrimeintel_x:9c2ef4c15885b5d227c3 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:worldcrimeintel_x:9c2ef4c15885b5d227c3 -->

### Text
Spain: Three Duarry Supercat high-performance rigid-hulled inflatable boats, designed for military use and each measuring six meters and weighing 640 kg, were stolen from the Navy during Flotex-26 maneuvers at El Retín beach in Barbate.
The theft, carried out overnight as military units were conducting coastal reconnaissance, also included 11–12 Navy specialized dry suits and tactical tracking equipment.
Investigation by the Guardia Civil suggests the boats, equipped with Yamaha 100-horsepower outboards, have likely been repurposed by drug trafficking groups operating between Cádiz and Morocco for fuel supply and rapid coastal landings.
These vessels’ Kevlar-reinforced hulls and stealthy coastal capabilities make them highly sought after by criminal organizations in the Strait of Gibraltar.

### Text
Türkiye: Police seized 1,700,000 synthetic pharmaceutical pills, identified as illicit drug-type synthetic pills, in a TIR during a traffic stop in Kocaeli.
Follow-up raids at addresses linked to the suspects uncovered 264 kg of precursor chemicals, enough to produce an estimated 3,000,000 additional synthetic pills, as well as 19 pill production machines.
The pills and precursor materials were found across multiple locations, including a clandestine pill manufacturing site in the Darıca and Gebze districts. Nine suspects were detained, with one already remanded in custody and legal proceedings ongoing for the others.

title: [RSS] Security White Paper 2016-003 - Authentication Methods
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:bc6716ac3b4bb28ae64f -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:bc6716ac3b4bb28ae64f -->

### Text
Lately, protecting data has become increasingly difficult task. Cyber-attacks have become one of the most serious threats to any organization. Companies and organizations are taking measures in order to defend their assets, and the authentication methods are an increasingly important security measure.
Authentication is the security term for verifying that the user is indeed who he claims to be. The procedure of confirming a user’s authenticity, is the action of comparing the provided credentials of the user against an existing database of validated identities.
However, since depending only on the use of simple credentials – or a single method of authentication in general – have lately proven to be highly unreliable, the use of multiple factors for the authentication process is highly recommended.

title: [RSS] UPDATED - Security White Paper 2017-001_v1_2 - DMARC — Defeating E-Mail Abuse
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:2d06479fe1a93eb87778 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:2d06479fe1a93eb87778 -->

### Text
DMARC is a mechanism to define a coherent e-mail policy that can effectively be used by both the sender and the receiver of the e-mail messages. The senders can list the authentication mechanisms they have put in place, and the receivers are informed what the sender suggests them to do, if the authentication fails on any message that
claims to originate from them.

title: [RSS] Security White Paper 2017-002_Detecting Lateral Movements in Windows Infrastructure
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:3e28b0b7aaf5211d4992 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:3e28b0b7aaf5211d4992 -->

### Text
Lateral movement techniques are widely used in sophisticated cyber-attacks in particular in Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs). An adversary uses these techniques to access other hosts from a compromised system and get access to sensitive resources, such as mailboxes, shared folders, or credentials. This white-paper provides guidelines to detect
the lateral movements exploiting NTLM and Kerberos protocols in a Windows 7 and 2008 based environments.

title: [RSS] Security White Paper 2017-004 - Mitigating Risks Related to Network Devices
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:7847560426dfb37c8410 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:7847560426dfb37c8410 -->

### Text
Network devices, such as routers, switches, or firewalls, are essential components of every IT infrastructure. All traffic has to go through several such network devices. Compromising network devices allows an adversary to steal sensitive data, corrupt communications, or disrupt activity of the targeted organization. The range of attacks against network devices has been growing for the past years, from exploitation of undocumented access to development of complex implants modifying the behavior of devices. The purpose of this white-paper is to provide recommendations on how to assess, prevent, and detect network devices
compromise.

In the last years we have seen an increasing use of PowerShell for malicious purposes. This was mainly caused by its powerfulness and lack of means to counter this kind of usage. title: [RSS] Security Guidance 22-002 - Hardening Signal
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:054e845000f2c9101277 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:054e845000f2c9101277 -->

### Text
Signal is a well-known, secure, encrypted instant messaging service developed by the non-profit Signal Technology Foundation and Signal Messenger LLC. It uses standard cellular telephone numbers as identifiers and all communications between Signal users are secured with end-to end encryption.
Staff of public and private organisations, including senior management, may be using Signal sometimes to quickly coordinate and exchange information on work-related matters. Signal groups may also have been set up for business continuity reasons in case corporate instant messaging tools become unavailable.
The following document provides clear and pragmatic recommendations for hardening the configuration of Signal apps. If you have suggestions that could help improve it, contact us at services@cert.europa.eu. We always appreciate constructive feedback.

### Text
On February 14, ENISA and CERT-EU made a joint publication strongly encouraging all EU- based organisations to implement a set of cybersecurity best practices.
Building on this joint publication, CERT-EU is making available the following specific imple- mentation recommendations. By applying these systematically, organisations can boost their cybersecurity defence and resilience. This would allow them to:
1. Improve their cybersecurity posture to fend off a wide range of attacks and limit the number of cybersecurity incidents.
2. Detect and react to cyber operations that may be carried off by sophisticated threat actors.

title: [RSS] Security Guidance 23-002 - Potential impact and risks of Generative AI in EUIBAs
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:5ce76874b7076fc4195d -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:5ce76874b7076fc4195d -->

### Text
Recently, there has been a substantial leap forward in the development of Generative AI technology. Additionally,
further rapid advancements are expected as researchers continue to push the boundaries of AI capabilities. At CERT-EU, we assess
that the development of Generative AI technology will continue and become quite common, and as such, it is crucial to embrace
this transformative innovation. The technology presents numerous opportunities, however, it also comes with risks. This guidance
aims at defining the position of CERT-EU towards Generative AI technology and provide an initial overview of this complex topic.
The most recent version adds a bit of information on indirect prompt-injection attacks and additional options for EUIBAs to use the technology.

title: [RSS] DDoS Overview and Response Guide
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:1770ebb7c1f06956fdb4 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:1770ebb7c1f06956fdb4 -->

### Text
The evolution of DDoS attack techniques and targets has been continuously followed in the past by the specialists ranging from large companies to security expert blogs. However, recently it has caught general attention due to several incidents that might mean a change of paradigm in the way such attacks have been addressed so far. Strategies to mitigate DDoS need to be adopted, and should focus initially on prevention, but eventually on designing multi-layered defense strategies. In this white-paper, CERT-EU has focused on procedures for securing IT infrastructure from threats against availability. The white-paper is based on proven DDoS identification and mitigation methods that can effectively and efficiently respond to DDoS attacks.

title: [RSS] Generative AI in Cybersecurity: Balancing Innovation and Risk
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:070a14fc0c4f351725fc -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_guidance_rss:070a14fc0c4f351725fc -->

### Text
The integration of generative AI into cybersecurity operations represents both unprecedented opportunity and emerging risk. While these technologies offer powerful capabilities for threat analysis, incident response, and security automation, they simultaneously introduce new attack vectors that adversaries are rapidly exploiting. The cybersecurity community faces a critical challenge: how to harness AI's defensive potential whilst maintaining robust protection against AI-enabled threats. Organisations across sectors are grappling with questions of responsible adoption, governance frameworks, and risk management strategies. In this guidance, CERT-EU examines the dual nature of generative AI in cybersecurity, providing practical recommendations for security teams seeking to balance innovation with vigilance. Drawing from operational experience and threat landscape analysis, this resource offers actionable insights for navigating the complex decision-making process surrounding AI adoption in security contexts.

### Text
On 17 March 2026, Microsoft updated one of its January 2026 security advisories related to a remote code execution vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint. Specifically, Microsoft raised the CVSS score and changed the FAQ section to indicate that the vulnerability could be exploited by an unauthenticated attacker. This vulnerability was added in the CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalogue on 18 March 2026.
Additionally, three further RCE flaws affecting Microsoft SharePoint were addressed in the March 2026 release.
CERT-EU strongly recommends updating SharePoint servers as soon as possible, prioritising internet-facing assets. CERT-EU also encourages IT administrators to take necessary remediation actions.

title: [RSS] 2026-005: High Vulnerability in the Linux Kernel ("Copy Fail")
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:certeu_advisories_rss:a19c2d96147faf086ec7 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:certeu_advisories_rss:a19c2d96147faf086ec7 -->

### Text
On 29 April 2026, a high local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel, tracked as CVE-2026-31431 and named "Copy Fail", was publicly disclosed.
The vulnerability affects every mainstream Linux distributions shipping a kernel built since 2017. A public proof-of-concept exploit has been released.
As of the date of this advisory, no distribution has shipped a fixed kernel package. The mainline fix was committed on 1 April 2026, but vendor updates are still pending across all major distributions. CERT-EU strongly recommends applying the interim mitigation immediately, prioritising Kubernetes nodes, and CI/CD runners exposed to untrusted workloads.

In Los Cedritos, two labs were seized; the first contained 400 liters of acetic acid and 1,200 kg of caustic soda.

We have lost 42 aircraft of which 24 are drones so that leaves us with 18 manned aircraft losses. Out of these three were friendly fire and 7 were from the rescue operation of the F-15E that was shot down over Iran.
24 aircraft losses spread out across over 10,200 air sorties striking over 13,000 individual targets. With nearly 40% of the aircraft lost from a single event, I don't think that's a sign of US power decline in the slightest.
Phillips P. OBrien (@PhillipsPOBrien)
Just sent out this post. US losses across the board fighting Iran, but particularly involving aircraft, are revealing worrying signs that US military efficiency is in decline and not what it was. The loss rates are many times higher than Vietnam and Desert Storm. A fish rots from the head down.

### Text
Germany: An attempted daytime hit targeted four members of the Remmo clan in Berlin’s Kreuzberg neighborhood, with a gunman firing five shots at their VW Passat from the median strip after alighting from a scooter driven by an accomplice.
Despite multiple bullet impacts on the vehicle, none of the occupants, aged 24, 32, 35, and 55, were injured. The attackers fled on the scooter, and police detained the car’s occupants shortly after, but the Remmo clan members refused to cooperate with investigators.
The case highlights ongoing violent conflicts between rival organized crime groups in Berlin.

### Text
We had our very own Vidal Baboon in a destroyer. Would cut/massacre your hair in the Sea Dart missile quarters. He would then hoover your head to ensure no loose hairs in the compartment which was a...surprising sensation
🤫No one onboard ever spoke of this part👀
John Ʌ Konrad V (@johnkonrad)
Navy ships have hundreds of crew, so there’s usually a trained barber—or at least one guy who can run clippers without creating a war crime.
Merchant ships have 25 people.
So you get TERRIBLE haircuts.
But I’ve never before seen one bad enough to trigger NATO Article 5.

<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:tom_sharpe_x:365c42d06cb3d8830da8 -->

### Text
The mania for ever bigger warships may be peaking with plans for US Navy battleships. But, even if the Trump Class don't happen, the USN will likely end up with smaller vessels...like 15 000 tons cruisers, in addition to an autonomous fleet.
Just like China, Russia, Japan, India, EU navies, Australia et al will have pretty big combatants for a while, and 'frigates' of today and tomorrow will not exactly be small.
Last, but hopefully not least, the UK will muddle along with its own mix of drones and warships in the 7,000 to 8,000 tons displacement range (T31s and T26s, which are cruiser-sized warships masquerading as frigates).
And let's face it the autonomous/alternately crewed missile vessels of the future will be at least the size of corvettes. So, warship admirers will have plenty to salivate over 😁
China's, the USA's, France's, and possibly the UK's supercarriers could still be here in 2070s, with more in the works. Unless a war causes an accelerated extinction.
PS: submarines are getting big too and will be so for decades to come, with SSNs al…

title: [RSS] Measures Against the “Russian Shadow Fleet”
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:swp_publications_en:a768cee3b33efbf29199 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:swp_publications_en:a768cee3b33efbf29199 -->

### Text
Only a Systematic Approach Can Ensure Effectiveness and Security
 The so-called Russian shadow fleet primarily serves to evade sanctions on oil exports and thereby finance its war of aggression against Ukraine. At the same time, it is increasingly emerging as a security risk with defence implications for Europe. Vessels associated with the shadow fleet are being linked with growing frequency to acts of espionage and sabotage, thereby posing a threat to German and European security as well as to the maritime environ­ment. In close coordination with its partners, the German government should pursue a sys­tematic approach to countering the shadow fleet effectively. The overarching objectives are to preserve the integrity of sanctions regimes, safeguard maritime security, and contain defence-related threats.

### Text
Something very interesting is happening in Europe right now.
France buys Swedish GlobalEye.
Sweden looks at French FDI frigates.
Germany shows growing interest in GlobalEye.
France just ordered additional Saab Giraffe radars.
This is starting to look far bigger than normal arms deals. 👀
For decades, Europe mostly bought American architecture:
AWACS
Patriot
Aegis
Link systems
US battle management
US cloud/network integration
The US didn’t just sell weapons.
It sold the nervous system of NATO.
France officially ordered Saab GlobalEye AEW&C aircraft with options for more.
France brings the nuclear umbrella.
Sweden brings the eyes and the network.
Together, they may be building the backbone of Europe’s future deterrence.” 😎
That is a massive signal.
France is not just another customer.
It is:
Europe’s leading military power
the EU’s only nuclear power after Brexit
a country obsessed with strategic sovereignty
Meanwhile Germany reportedly has GlobalEye in “pole position” for its future AEW&C requirement. �

<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:worldcrimeintel_x:94fe21c04717c726d1db -->

### Text
Puerto Rico: Authorities identified a criminal organization trafficking cocaine from the Dominican Republic using high-speed boats departing from Sabana del Mar and Miches.
In a recent operation, Border Patrol [CBP] intercepted a twin-engine vessel without navigation lights 19 nautical miles north of San Juan after its crew ignored verbal commands. Surveillance footage showed the suspects throwing bundles overboard before the vessel sank due to structural failure.
Five bales, later recovered by agents, tested positive for cocaine with a total weight of 178 kg. Three Dominican nationals were apprehended in connection with the smuggling attempt, linked to a broader investigation initiated in March by the Drug Enforcement Administration [DEA].

Indiens Bemühungen um eigenständige und wettbewerbsfähige Weltraum­fähigkeiten sind jedoch trotz erleichterter Investitions- und Regulierungsbedingungen noch nicht umfassend vorangeschritten. My gratitude and thanks to the crew of the USS Gerald R. Ford after nearly a year at sea.

title: [X] Canada: Quebec provincial police issued an arrest warrant for Denis Beaupré, linked to the Hells Angels, for the first-degree murder of Charalambos Theologou, aka Bobby The Greek, the alleged leader of the Chomedey Greeks criminal gr…
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:worldcrimeintel_x:667f9569df2ea0aa8921 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:worldcrimeintel_x:667f9569df2ea0aa8921 -->

### Text
Canada: Quebec provincial police issued an arrest warrant for Denis Beaupré, linked to the Hells Angels, for the first-degree murder of Charalambos Theologou, aka Bobby The Greek, the alleged leader of the Chomedey Greeks criminal group, shot dead in a crowded Starbucks in Laval in October 2025.
The attack, captured on surveillance cameras, involved two masked shooters entering the café, firing multiple rounds at Theologou and his associates in less than 15 seconds, and then fleeing. An undercover officer was present during the shooting as part of ongoing surveillance.
Beaupré is believed to have disappeared just days after the murder and may also have been killed. Police searched a rural property in Vankleek Hill, Ontario, using canine units and drones, seeking evidence or Beaupré’s remains.

title: [RSS] The Destabilising Role of the United Arab Emirates in African Conflicts
summary:
<!-- MAGIC_PAWS_INGEST_ID:swp_publications_en:c80569e0d384624efd64 -->
<!-- VOODOO_INGEST_ID:swp_publications_en:c80569e0d384624efd64 -->

### Text
The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has become one of the most aggressive external actors in African conflicts, from Ethiopia, Libya and Somalia to Sudan. The leadership in Abu Dhabi obstinately denies its support for belligerents, yet it has maintained it even during the US-Israeli war against Iran – despite the serious repercussions for the UAE. Its role impedes efforts at conflict resolution and exacerbates humanitarian crises and regional instability. It undermines Europe’s interest in reliable trade routes, the prevention of forced displacement and regional integration. Germany and its Euro­pean partners should accord much greater weight to the UAE’s destabilising actions in their bilateral relations, criticise them more explicitly and consider sanctions. The context of the war with Iran, as well as tensions between the UAE and Saudi Arabia, offers an opening for a change in policy in Abu Dhabi.

### Text
Mexico: Authorities uncovered a sophisticated fuel theft operation in Santa Catarina, Nuevo León, where a clandestine tunnel connected a private property to an 18-inch Pemex pipeline.
The tunnel’s entrance was hidden within a maritime metal container on the property, which also housed a logistics hub with offices, storage areas, and vehicle fleets.
Specialized equipment, including a welded nipple, high-pressure hose, and shut-off valve, enabled direct extraction of 205,418 liters of stolen fuel. The operation resulted in the seizure of 23 tractor-trailers, 10 tankers, a crane, seven dry boxes, three vehicles.

### Text
Italy: The extradition of Dave Turmel, alleged leader of the Blood Family Mafia, to Canada has been annulled by an Italian court following an appeal.
Turmel, detained in Rome since March 2025 after 610 days on the run, remains incarcerated while Italian courts reconsider the extradition process from scratch.
He faces multiple charges in Canada, including gangsterism, drug trafficking, conspiracy, armed assault, kidnappings, and orchestrating violent acts during a drug war against the Hells Angels in Quebec. The timeline for a final decision on his extradition remains uncertain.

summary:

### Zeit (UTC)
2026-05-12T15:07:37.000Z

### Link
https://nitter.poast.org/WorldCrimeIntel/status/2054216935164805221#m

### Text
South Africa: The Madlanga Commission heard Colonel Francois Steyn of the Hawks about a shipment of 750 kg of cocaine smuggled from Brazil to Johannesburg in July 2021, using the rip-on/rip-off concealment method within a container of vehicle body parts destined for Scania South Africa.
The cocaine was hidden at the port of Santos, Brazil, shipped to Durban, then trucked to Johannesburg. Syndicates exploited duplicate seals and the huge traffic at Durban port, where only containers flagged by intelligence are checked, Steyn noted up to 4,500 containers enter daily.
At Yellow Jersey Logistics, a traffic officer was blocked from searching the truck without a warrant, which then proceeded under private security escort to Scania.
On arrival, officers allegedly removed the cocaine bags, loaded them onto an unmarked vehicle, and mishandled the scene, raising suspicions of police interference and corruption.

title: [SOCIAL] Spain: The court has revoked the drug trafficking indictment against former head of the U…
summary:
### Quelle
World Crime Intel (X) (worldcrimeintel_x)

### Plattform
x

### Zeit (UTC)
2026-05-12T13:08:03.000Z

### Link
https://nitter.poast.org/WorldCrimeIntel/status/2054186845521515004#m

### Text
Spain: The court has revoked the drug trafficking indictment against former head of the UDEF (Unit for Economic and Fiscal Crime) in Madrid, Óscar Sánchez, due to incomplete investigation and lack of individualized evidence.
Sánchez, accused of collaborating closely with a criminal network led by Ignacio Torán and facilitating the import of over 73 tons of cocaine in 39 maritime containers between 2020 and 2024, remains in pretrial detention.
Both Sánchez and Torán are alleged to have participated in a network under investigation for international cocaine trafficking and money laundering, with authorities reporting the seizure of over 20 million euros in cash at Sánchez’s residence and office.
The court emphasized that further investigative steps, some with international scope, are needed before resuming prosecution, particularly regarding the specific role of each accused within the organization and the origins of their illicit proceeds.

title: [RSS] Maßnahmen gegen die »russische Schattenflotte«
summary:
### Quelle
SWP – Offizielle Publikationen DE (swp_publications_de)

### Plattform
rss

### Zeit (UTC)
2026-05-12T10:01:00.000Z

### Link
https://www.swp-berlin.org/publikation/massnahmen-gegen-die-russische-schattenflotte

### Text
Nur ein systematischer Ansatz kann für Effektivität und Sicherheit sorgen
Russlands sogenannte Schattenflotte dient in erster Linie der Sanktionsumgehung für den Ölexport und damit der Finanzierung seines Angriffskrieges gegen die Ukraine. Sie stellt jedoch vermehrt auch ein verteidigungsrelevantes Sicherheitsrisiko für Europa dar. Schiffe der Schattenflotte werden zunehmend mit Spionage- und Sabo­tage­­akten in Verbindung gebracht, sie bedrohen deutsche und europäische Sicherheit und die maritime Umwelt. Die Bundesregierung sollte gemeinsam mit ihren Partnern einen systematischen Ansatz verfolgen, um wirksam gegen die Schattenflotte vor­zugehen. Ziel ist es, Sanktionsregime aufrechtzuerhalten, die maritime Sicherheit zu gewährleisten und verteidigungsrelevante Bedrohungen einzudämmen.

### Text
Bolivia: Kleber Nobrega Pereira, alias Kekeu, identified as one of the leaders of the Comando Vermelho criminal organization, was captured in Cotoca, Santa Cruz, by the Special Force to Fight Drug Trafficking (Felcn) and the Intelligence and Special Operations Group (GIOE).
He was subject to a red notice from Interpol and wanted in Brazil for organized crime, money laundering, drug and arms trafficking, violent homicides, corruption of minors, and other illicit activities, with operations spanning Salvador and the south and southwest regions of Bahia.
In the same operation, his wife, alleged to be in charge of the group’s financial transactions and money laundering network in Bahia and Rio de Janeiro, was also arrested.
Both were transferred to Puerto Quijarro for official handover to Federal Police of Brazil under bilateral cooperation protocols.

title: [RSS] Resilience as a Response to Russian Threats in Space
summary:
### Quelle
SWP – Official Publications EN (swp_publications_en)

### Plattform
rss

### Zeit (UTC)
2026-05-11T13:23:59.000Z

### Link
https://www.swp-berlin.org/en/publication/resilience-as-a-response-to-russian-threats-in-space

### Text
Policy Options for Germany and Europe
Russian attacks and threat posturing are increasingly extending into space as well. This poses risks to infrastructure in Europe, which is becoming ever more dependent on satellite systems. This affects civilian services such as navigation, but also Europe’s defence capabilities. The question is therefore increasingly how such manoeuvres by Russia should be countered, and whether weapons systems should be deployed in space as a deterrent. Europe’s armed forces are far more dependent on space systems than the Russian armed forces. Given this asymmetry, Germany and its partners should focus primarily on the resilience of their satellite systems.

One suspect, a Syrian national, was hospitalized, the other died in the blaze, and minor injuries were reported among soldiers and civilians. The raid at the factory resulted in the seizure of drug manufacturing equipment and a large quantity of unspecified narcotics.

Der Neuansatz der Vereinten Nationen zur Kontrolle des Staatszerfalls
Haiti leidet seit Jahrzehnten unter zahlreichen humanitären, politischen, wirtschaftlichen und sicherheitspolitischen Krisen, die einander überlagern und verheerende Folgen für die lokale Bevölkerung haben. Bisherige Stabilisierungsversuche waren kaum erfolgreich. Die 5.500 Mann starke sogenannte »Einheit zur Bekämpfung von Banden« (Gang Suppression Force, GSF) hat am 1. April 2026 die Nachfolge der von Kenia geführten Multinationalen Sicherheitsunterstützungsmission (MSS) angetreten. Es ist die zwölfte Friedens- und Stabilisierungsmission, die Haiti seit 1990 erlebt. Die GSF wird als VN-mandatierte Mission geführt und operiert vor allem mit zunächst 800 Militärkräften aus dem Tschad. Damit unternehmen die Vereinten Nationen einen gewagten Versuch, die Bekämpfung der organisierten Kriminalität zum zentralen Ansatzpunkt ihrer Missionen nach Kapitel VII der VN-Charta zu machen – und das i…

summary:
### Quelle
SWP – Offizielle Publikationen DE (swp_publications_de)

### Plattform
rss

### Zeit (UTC)
2026-05-08T10:45:07.000Z

### Link
https://www.swp-berlin.org/publikation/frieden-in-der-krise-konfliktbearbeitung-am-horn-von-afrika

### Text
Das System internationaler Konfliktbearbeitung befindet sich in einer tiefen Krise. Entsprechende Instrumente wie UN-Friedensmissionen werden abgezogen, normative und machtpolitische Voraussetzungen wie eine US-garantierte internationale Ordnung gelten nicht mehr, und bisherige Abkommen haben zu oft eine Vertiefung gewaltsamer autoritärer Systeme statt deren Transformation bewirkt. Am Horn von Afrika zeigt sich diese Krise besonders deutlich. Stabilität brachten dort weder international erzwungene Friedensabkommen wie in Südsudan noch solche, die wie in Sudan 2020 praktisch ohne Mediation zwischen bewaffneten Akteuren ausgehandelt wurden. Regierungen wie die äthiopische haben Friedensprozesse vielmehr regelmäßig instrumentalisiert, um die eigene Herrschaft zu sichern. Jüngere Vermittlungsprozesse sind primär eine Funktion regionaler Rivalitäten und diplomatischer Interessen von beteiligten externen Akteu­ren, wie in Sudan seit Kriegsbeginn…

### Text
The US-Israel war against Iran and the standoff in the Strait of Hormuz are reshaping trade and transport links as well as changing regional connectivity across Eurasia. Disruptions to shipping and energy flows in the Strait, through which a fifth of global oil and gas trade passes in peacetime, are prompting regional actors to seek alternatives and creating openings for new transport corridors. Turkey is moving quickly to capitalise on this shift. Ankara is presenting itself as a relatively secure hub for trade, transport, and energy, turning connectivity into an instrument of strategic autonomy and regional influence.

As maritime routes become more vulnerable and fragmentation in the Middle East deepens, Turkey is increasingly focusing on three areas: energy corridors, air connectivity, and the South Caucasus.
